name: Southwest Meatball Supper
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 10-inch skillet, heat 1 tbsp. oil over medium high heat. Add onion and chili powder; cook about 5 minutes, stirring frequently until onion is tender and well coated with chili powder. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
2. Spoon onion into a large bowl. Wipe out the skillet.
3. Add ground beef, bread crumbs, egg, and salt to onion in the bowl; blend well using hands or a wooden spoon.
4. Shape the mixture into 1-inch balls.
5. To the skillet, add the remaining 2 tbsp. oil; heat over medium high heat.
6. Add meatballs and cook about 12 minutes until well browned on all sides and cooked through.
7. Stir in taco sauce and tomato; bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes to blend flavors.
8. To serve: Arrange a bed of shredded lettuce over a platter. Spoon the meatball mixture over the lettuce; sprinkle with cheese if desired. Garnish with avocado slices and basil sprigs if desired, and serve with taco chips. Makes 4 servings.
